title: centaurea jacea subsp. ruscinonensis
intro: Centaurea jacea subsp. ruscinonensis, also known as Ruscin knapweed, is a subspecies of the knapweed plant, which is a perennial herb belonging to the Asteraceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help soothe wounds, reduce pain, and combat infections. The health benefits of Centaurea jacea subsp. ruscinonensis include its ability to alleviate digestive issues, relieve fever, and support the immune system, making it a valuable herb for maintaining overall well-being. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used externally to treat skin conditions and wounds, or taken internally to support digestive health and boost the immune system.
